findings.where: /trips (Past section) + /trips/[slug] — src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:9-15; src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:35-44 (mode forced 'planning' when !active) and :76-80 (ModePill rendered only `{#if active}`); src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.svelte:117-135 (Past card: title+dates only, no state cue); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+page.svelte and src/lib/shell/components/ContextRail.svelte (zero closeout/archive/settle mentions — grep verified); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte:54-70 (sole closeout entry, owner-gated); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.server.ts:8-10 (non-owner redirect)
findings.what: Scenario 38 walk: the day after end_date, isTripActive flips false, the mode pill disappears, nav reverts to the 5-tab planning chrome, and the trip drops to a dimmed 'Past' card on /trips. Nothing anywhere says 'trip over — settle, close out, archive': not the trips list, not the trip overview, not the ContextRail. The only door is More → Closeout, which is owner-gated chrome AND an owner-gated load; a non-owner member has no post-trip surface at all (no settle nudge, nothing to review). Settle Up exists but only as a button inside Money (expenses/+page.svelte:163-164) with no end-of-trip prompt.
findings.why: Charter D5: this exact silent revert is the pre-agreed P1 vision gap — fails V3 (trip day→record seam drops the closing sequence entirely) and V2 for non-owner members (the 'group moment' of settle/memory review has no surface). Code confirms there is no third derived state: TripViewMode is a binary 'planning'|'trip' (activation.ts:1).
findings.fix: Per charter D5 this is feature-sized: Phase 3 routes to grill→PRD, not a quick issue. The PRD should add a derived wrapping-up state (end < today && !archived) that re-skins the trip home + trips-list Past card (quiet 'needs closeout' affordance), opens settle to all members, and leads into the existing closeout wizard.

findings.title: isTripActive uses UTC while every other date predicate uses trip timezone — Trip Mode dies mid-evening on the last day
findings.where: src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:11 (`now.toISOString().split('T')[0]` — UTC calendar date; TripDateInfo at :3-7 doesn't even carry timezone) vs src/lib/shell/trip-time.ts:44-47 (tz-aware tripToday) used by src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.server.ts:30-47 for On-trip/Past grouping; consumers: src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:35-44, src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:20 (offline gating)
findings.what: Two contradictory 'is the trip happening' predicates ship side by side. For a US-timezone trip (e.g. America/Chicago, UTC-5/6), UTC rolls over ~6-7pm local: on the final day of the trip, at dinner time, isTripActive flips false — the mode pill vanishes, mode is hard-forced to 'planning' (AppShell.svelte:40 ignores userOverride when !active), bottom nav swaps to planning tabs, and /now and /today lose every chrome edge (now has no +page.server.ts guard, so it becomes URL-only). Meanwhile /trips still shows the trip under 'On trip' via the tz-aware predicate. Mirror bug at trip start: Trip Mode activates the evening before day one. Documents' on-trip behavior skews identically.
findings.why: M3 — Trip Mode (Now/Today/Add) becomes a shipped-but-unreachable feature during the trip's final evening while the trip is factually ongoing in its own timezone; guard-vs-chrome contradiction between activation.ts and trips/+page.server.ts. Charter severity bar: mid-trip dead end on a critical path = P1. The last evening is exactly when day-wrap surfaces matter.
findings.fix: One-line shape: make isTripActive take the trip's timezone and compare against tripToday(tripTz(trip), now) instead of now.toISOString(); extend TripDateInfo with `timezone`. Add a unit case in activation.test.ts for an evening-of-last-day in a negative-UTC-offset zone.

findings.title: Archive publish gate contradicts settings copy: delay measured from end_date not archiving, bypassed entirely once archived, and trips auto-publish without closeout
findings.where: src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:28-34 (publishDate = end_date + archive_publish_after_days; gate is `if (!trip.archived && now < publishDate)`) vs src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.svelte:126 ('a public read-only link is generated after the trip is archived') and :153-155 ('Publish delay (days after archiving)'); token actually minted at enable-time in settings/+page.server.ts:106-109; src/lib/portability/archive-view.ts:36-38 (consideredItems includes status='planned')
findings.what: The label promises two things the code does not do. (1) 'Days after archiving' is computed from end_date — and if the trip IS archived, the delay is skipped completely, so in the normal closeout flow the link is live the second the owner taps 'Finish & Archive'; the delay knob has no effect on the path it claims to govern. (2) 'Generated after the trip is archived' — the token is generated when the checkbox is enabled, and the page goes public at end_date+delay even if closeout/archiving never happens; an owner who enabled the toggle pre-trip and never closed out has their full planned itinerary (titles, locations, addresses) publicly rendered under 'What we considered' (archive-view.ts maps leftover 'planned' into consideredItems) 7 days after the trip ends, without ever consenting to publish.
findings.why: M3/guard-vs-chrome — the shipped publish-delay control is unreachable-in-effect on the normal flow, and the documented publish precondition ('after archived') is not enforced by the gate. Owner-expectation breach is code-verified, not vibes.
findings.fix: Pick one model and make copy match code: gate on `trip.archived` as the hard precondition (never public before closeout), and compute publishDate from the archive timestamp (store archived_at) so the delay does what its label says. Update settings copy accordingly.

findings.title: Publishing has no share moment: finishCloseout mints the public token silently and the only surfacing is a relative-path <code> block buried in Settings
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.server.ts:111-118 (token generated, then redirect(303, `/trips/${slug}`) with no UI), src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.svelte:167-174 (`/archive/{token}` — not an , no copy button, no origin prefix; grep confirms this is the ONLY render of public_share_token app-wide), src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+page.svelte (overview shows no archive cue post-redirect)
findings.what: Scenario 35 walk ('share the trip with grandma'): owner finishes the closeout wizard → lands on the trip overview, which looks identical to before — no 'your archive link is ready'. To find the link: More → Settings → Public Archive card → read a path fragment like /archive/x7k... rendered as inline code; a novice must select the text by hand and prepend the domain themselves to produce a working URL. If archive_enabled was never toggled (default false, trips/new/+page.server.ts:60), finishCloseout mints no token at all and Settings shows nothing — with no hint during closeout that enabling the archive was a prerequisite to having anything to share.
findings.why: M4 — expected-but-missing link at the exact moment the artifact is created; the closeout wizard's final act produces a public URL the owner is never shown. V1 consequence: the 'share the record' job collapses into manual URL construction.
findings.fix: After finishCloseout, land on (or toast into) a publish confirmation that shows the absolute URL with a copy/native-share button; in Settings, render the share URL as a full absolute link with copy-to-clipboard. Surface an 'enable public archive?' choice inside the closeout summary step so the decision happens at the seam, not in buried settings.

findings.title: Archived state is invisible and irreversible; Settings' archiveTrip action is dead code
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.server.ts:124-150 (archiveTrip action — repo-wide grep finds zero forms posting `?/archiveTrip`); `archived` is consumed only by src/lib/trip-mode/activation.ts:10, src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/members/+page.server.ts:153-154 (join links disabled) and src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:33; `archived: false` is written only at create (trips/new:62, import:52, clone:80) — no unarchive setter exists anywhere; settings/+page.svelte renders no archived indicator and no un-archive control
findings.what: Once 'Finish & Archive Trip' is tapped, `archived=true` forever: no UI reads it back (the trip renders exactly like a planning trip — no banner, no read-only state, Past grouping is date-based not archived-based), and no UI can unset it. Consequences are silent and permanent: Trip Mode can never activate again even if the owner extends dates via Settings (activation.ts:10 short-circuits on archived), and join links are dead (members:154) with no explanation visible anywhere. Separately, the archiveTrip server action — the archive-without-closeout path — is shipped but unreachable: no button posts to it, leaving the full day-by-day wizard as the only road to archived=true.
findings.why: M2 — one-way street with no way back and no error path (the user can't even see the state they're stuck in); M3 — archiveTrip is a dead action. An accidental archive's only 'workaround' is cloning the trip, which loses data (see clone finding).
findings.fix: Render archived state on the trip (overview banner + Settings status row) and add an owner-gated 'Reopen trip' action that sets archived=false. Either wire a 'Archive now (skip closeout)' button to the existing archiveTrip action in the Settings archive card, or delete the dead action.

findings.title: Clone silently drops every flight and flattens all idea/done statuses into day-less 'planned' orphans
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/clone/+page.svelte:14 (`itemTypes = ['lodging','transportation','activity','meal','note']` — 'flight' missing despite ItemType union at src/lib/itinerary/types.ts:38 and the load counting them at clone/+page.server.ts:19-22); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/clone/+page.server.ts:153 (`status: 'planned'` hardcoded for every cloned item), :141 (`day: dayIdMap.get(item.day) || ''`), :138-164 (`requires_booking` omitted from the create payload)
findings.what: Scenario 36 walk ('start next year's trip from this one'): (1) Flights can never be cloned — the type checkbox list omits 'flight', so a trip with 4 flights shows no flight row and the server filter silently excludes them; nothing tells the user. (2) Every source item is force-created as status 'planned': parking-lot ideas (the most valuable clone payload for a repeat trip) arrive as planned items with day='' — invisible on every surface, since parking lots filter status='unplanned' (days/[dayId]/+page.server.ts:58, phases/[phaseId]/+page.server.ts:112) and day views filter by day id; the swipe deck is the only surface that can ever show them. With 'Clone phases & days' unchecked, ALL items become day-less planned orphans. (3) requires_booking isn't copied, so cloned lodging/transport never appears in the booking Smart List (needsBooking predicate).
findings.why: M3 — flight cloning is shipped-but-unreachable (server supports the type; UI never offers it) and cloned ideas land in an unreachable state no view queries; V3 — the planning→next-trip seam silently drops and corrupts data. Checklist reset/skip behavior is intentional (ADR-0003 §7) and NOT part of this finding.
findings.fix: Add 'flight' to the itemTypes array; preserve source status for unplanned items (or map done/considered→unplanned as 'ideas again'), keep day only for planned items; copy requires_booking (re-defaulting booked=false is correct). Add a clone fidelity unit test over one item of each type/status.

findings.title: Closeout never reviews parking-lot ideas, contradicting the documented wizard contract; the archive then drops them while relabeling skipped-planned items as 'considered'
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/closeout/+page.svelte:26-28 (`itemsForDay` filters i.day === day.id — unplanned items, day='', never enter the wizard) and :74-111 (wizard = day cards → summary; no phase-end step exists), :123-124 (summary's own label admits leftovers: 'Skipped (stayed planned)'); src/lib/portability/archive-view.ts:35-38 (doneItems=done; consideredItems = planned OR considered — unplanned excluded entirely); contract: CONTEXT.md Closeout glossary ('At the end of each phase, reviews unplanned items: keep for archive (marked considered) or remove, with an option to bulk auto-consider')
findings.what: The wizard walks planned/day-assigned items only. The phase-end unplanned review promised by CONTEXT.md (a live doc) — and assumed to exist by V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD.md:129 ('a goal-review step after the per-phase unplanned review') — does not exist in code, and SPEC_BACKLOG.md's Archive & Portability section does not defer it. Net effect on the record: every parking-lot idea ('the restaurant we never got to') vanishes from the public archive's 'What we considered', while items the owner explicitly skipped during the wizard (left planned) are silently presented to the public as 'considered'. Goals review at closeout is documented-deferred (V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD.md:34-35, 241-244) and Memory review rides the shelved TRIP_MEMORY_PRD — both cited, neither flagged.
findings.why: V3 — the trip→record seam drops the ideas half of the trip's history, and misfiles the skipped half. Also a live-doc lie for the doc-hygiene appendix (CONTEXT.md describes a wizard step that doesn't exist).
findings.fix: Add the documented phase-end review step to the wizard: list that phase's unplanned items with keep-for-archive (→considered) / remove, plus the bulk auto-consider button; stop mapping leftover 'planned' into the archive's considered bucket once the wizard can actually resolve them (or keep it as fallback but resolve statuses at finishCloseout).

findings.title: Export 'JSON backup' omits the money ledger, goals, and documents — the kept record is partial
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/export/+server.ts:19-35 (fetches only phases/days/items + manual checklists + budget) and src/lib/portability/export.ts:15-90 (TripExport shape: no expenses, no settlements, no members, no trip_goals, no documents, no votes/comments); chrome promise: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte:118-119 ('Export — Download trip as JSON backup'); scenario 37
findings.what: The More card sells export as the trip's backup, but the payload is itinerary-plus-budget-categories only. Who paid what, settlements, goals ('did we do what we set out to'), and uploaded confirmation documents are all absent — so the portable record of a closed trip permanently lacks its financial history and aspirations the moment the PB instance goes away. Checklist assignee-stripping and item-scoped-list exclusion are decided in ADR-0003 §7 (cited, not flagged); nothing in SPEC_BACKLOG.md or any PRD defers expenses/goals/documents export — the Archive & Portability backlog section contains only the archive-stays-plan-only note.
findings.why: V1/V3 — 'keep the record' is a charter pillar; a backup that drops the ledger sends the group back to Splitwise screenshots for any money question after the trip, and the seam trip→record loses goals and artifacts. Import (trips/import) consumes this same shape, so clone-via-export round-trips lose the same data.
findings.fix: Extend buildTripExport with expenses+settlements (member references serialized as display names, mirroring the day_date/phase_name denormalization pattern) and trip goals; bump _waypoint_version to 2 and keep import backward-compatible. Documents (binary) can be a documented exclusion in the export UI copy if intentionally out of scope.

findings.title: Public archive is a cul-de-sac: no path from the recap to Waypoint or to making the trip your own
findings.where: src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.svelte:120-122 (footer 'Built with Waypoint' is plain text — no <a>; page's only outbound link is the optional external photo_album_url at :47-61); scenario 39
findings.what: Scenario 39 walk ('share the recap with family so they can do the trip themselves'): a non-member visitor reaches a polished read-only itinerary and then — nothing. No link to the app root or login, no 'plan your own trip', and no clone-this-itinerary path; the visitor's only option is retyping the itinerary into their own tools. Not deferred anywhere: SPEC_BACKLOG's Archive & Portability backlog and V4_GROUP_INPUT_PRD's out-of-scope list cover archive content (plan-only, goals summary deferred), not visitor egress.
findings.why: M4 — expected-but-missing link on a charter-eligible public front-door surface (charter §6 makes /archive/[token] findings-eligible); the charter's own scenario 39 asks 'does any path lead from archive → their own trip?' and the code answer is no.
findings.fix: Minimal: make the footer an Built with Waypoint. The fuller 'use this as a template for your own trip' (token-gated clone into a new account) is feature-sized — Phase 3 PRD candidate, not a quick issue.

findings.title: No +error.svelte anywhere — archive/membership failures dead-end on SvelteKit's unstyled default page
findings.where: src/routes/ (find for +error.svelte returns zero files); src/routes/archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:21,25,34; src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+layout.server.ts:12,26
findings.what: The app has no +error.svelte at any level. Every thrown error() renders SvelteKit's bare default page: unstyled white screen, no Waypoint branding, no design tokens, no nav chrome, no link anywhere. Concrete hits on the public front door: /archive/[token] throws 404 'Archive not found' on a bad/rotated token and 404 'Archive not yet published' during the configurable publish-delay window — a state owners will routinely create by sharing the link early. In-app: a non-member or Departed Member (tombstoned per #133, bookmark still live) opening a trip URL gets raw '403 You are not a member of this trip'; bad slugs/dayIds/itemIds get raw 404s.
findings.why: M2 one-way street: every error path exits the app with no way back. Charter P1 bucket is explicit — 'front-door (invite/join/archive) failure'. Invite and join handle their failure states in-page (invite/[code]/+page.server.ts status enum; join/[token] same pattern), but archive — the one front door for non-members ('share with grandma', scenario 35) — throws. Grandma's journey ends on a developer error page; her recourse is texting the owner (V1, old stack). Designer lens: the single most off-design-system surface in the product is the one shown at the moment of failure.
findings.fix: Add a root src/routes/+error.svelte styled with design tokens (paper bg, Fraunces heading, friendly copy per status: 404 vs 403) with a 'Go to your trips' / 'Open Waypoint' action; optionally a trip-level +error.svelte that keeps trip chrome for in-trip 404s. For /archive/[token], prefer in-page states like invite/join (not-found vs not-yet-published with 'check back after {date}').

findings.title: Mode toggle does not exist between 900–1279px — active trips lock into Trip Mode with planning surfaces unreachable
findings.where: src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte:75-84 (mobile div incl. ModePill is md-desktop:hidden), :87-103 (desktop branch has no pill); src/lib/shell/components/SideRail.svelte:67-79 (pill wrapper is 'hidden lg-desktop:flex'); src/lib/shell/components/ContextRail.svelte:70 (lg-desktop:flex only); src/routes/layout.css:58-59 (md-desktop=900px, lg-desktop=1280px)
findings.what: On an active trip the mobile ModePill lives in the md-desktop:hidden div, and the SideRail's pill is hidden until lg-desktop (1280px). In the 900–1279px band (iPad Air/11" landscape at 1180/1194, half-screen laptop windows) there is no mode toggle anywhere. Mode defaults to 'trip' (AppShell.svelte:36-40), so the SideRail shows only Now/Today/Add/Docs: Itinerary, Money, Members, and More (settings, inbox, closeout, export, print) are unreachable except by typing URLs. Only partial mitigation: the Add sheet deep-links to /items/new and /expenses?action=add (src/lib/trip-mode/components/AddSheet.svelte:19-29). Not documented anywhere — design-system.md:129-130 describes both breakpoints without mentioning the pill, and SPEC_BACKLOG has no entry.
findings.why: M3 shipped-but-unreachable / M4 missing affordance; charter scenario 40 (switch modes and back) fails mechanically at this width, and it's a mid-trip dead end per the P1 bar: checking the budget mid-trip on an iPad → impossible → Splitwise (V1). D3 calls the mode pill 'a working door' for future-day replanning; at tablet width the door isn't on the wall.
findings.fix: Render the mode pill in the SideRail at md-desktop too (icon-rail variant: compact icon button or move it into AppShell's desktop branch above the content column). One-line class change on SideRail.svelte:68 ('hidden lg-desktop:flex' → 'flex') plus md-desktop styling.

findings.title: Auth gate drops the destination: deep links die at /login and resurface at /trips
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts:5-7 (redirect(303, '/login') with no return param); src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.server.ts:46 (verifyOTP hardcodes redirect to /claim); src/routes/(app)/claim/+page.server.ts:38 (falls through to /trips)
findings.what: Any unauthenticated (or session-expired) request to an (app) route redirects to /login with no ?redirectTo. After OTP verification the flow is hardcoded login → /claim → /trips. So 'look at the hotel I added: …/trips/paris-26/items/abc' pasted in the group text lands the recipient on the trips list with the item reference gone; a PWA user whose PB token expired mid-trip taps their home-screen icon or a bookmarked day and ends up at the trips list instead. The login page itself handles email correction well ('Use a different email', login/+page.svelte:117) — only the destination is lost.
findings.why: M4 expected-but-missing link (return-to). V2-adjacent: the member who followed a specific artifact link has to re-derive where it was — or asks in the group text ('which day was that on?'), which is the old stack. Designer lens: orientation loss at the exact moment a non-technical user is most fragile (mid-auth). Affects scenario 44 (land in the app and find the right one) for the multi-trip case.
findings.fix: Carry the original URL: (app)/+layout.server.ts redirect to /login?redirectTo={url.pathname}; verifyOTP redirects to /claim?redirectTo=…; claim's fall-through honors it (validate same-origin path). Invite/join flows already keep context in-page and need no change.

findings.title: Profile (/account) is reachable only from the trips-list avatar — invisible from inside any trip
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.svelte:36-43 (sole /account link in the app — grep confirms); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte (Inbox/Closeout/Settings/Clone/Export/Print/Offline cards, no Profile); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/members/+page.svelte:96 (own row offers no identity edit); sign-out likewise only at trips/+page.svelte:28-35
findings.what: /account (avatar upload + display name) is linked exactly once: the 32px avatar chip on the trips index NavBar. Inside a trip — where avatars actually appear (vote stacks, members list, comments) and where a new joiner is deposited — there is no path to it. The More tab, the natural 'everything else' surface, lists seven cards but not Profile; your own row on Members isn't a link. Sign out is equally trip-list-only. A user who lives inside one trip (the normal case for an invited friend) must discover that backing all the way out and tapping a tiny avatar chip is how you set your photo.
findings.why: M4 expected-but-missing link; charter scenario 41 ('change my name/avatar — /account reachability from where you'd expect') fails everywhere except the app root. Designer lens: AVATARS_PRD positions the avatar as identity shown 'wherever a member appears', but the system offers no edit affordance at any of those appearances. D2 compounds it: the post-join landing is a trip page, so the first five minutes — exactly when someone would set their photo — has no door to it.
findings.fix: Add a Profile card (avatar thumbnail + name) at the top of the More tab, and make your own row on Members link to /account. Both are additive links, no IA restructure.

findings.title: Settings page renders the full owner UI — including the danger zone — to travelers and viewers, rejecting them only after submit
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.server.ts:6-9 (load returns trip+membership, no role gate); src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.svelte (zero references to membership/role — full edit form, archive, delete rendered unconditionally); post-hoc 403s at +page.server.ts:38,67-68,133-134; entry: more/+page.svelte Settings card ('Trip details, slug, auto-approve, danger zone') shown to all roles
findings.what: More → Settings is visible to every role, and the page renders everything live for everyone: editable title/dates/timezone form, auto-approve toggle, archive action, and a Delete-trip button. Role enforcement exists only inside the form actions ('Only trip owners can change settings/delete the trip/archive a trip'), so a traveler or viewer discovers their actual permissions by filling out a form and being slapped with a 403 banner. Contrast: the same More page correctly hides Inbox/Closeout/Clone behind isOwnerOrCoOwner, and TripTabs.svelte (dead code, imported nowhere — superseded by per-page SubTabs) shows the older pattern of role-aware tab lists.
findings.why: V2: a contributor who tries to help (fix the timezone, correct an end date) is invited in by live affordances and then rejected — the reliable lesson a non-technical friend takes from one post-hoc 403 is 'don't touch anything', which silences them everywhere else. Novice-lens corollary of the charter quality bar: if it is visibly a button, it exists. Designer lens: role gating is applied at three different layers (card visibility on More, nothing on Settings render, action-time on submit) — no consistent system.
findings.fix: Gate at render like More already does: non-owners get a read-only summary (title, dates, timezone) with no danger zone and a 'Only owners can edit settings' note — or hide the Settings card for viewers entirely. Delete the unused TripTabs.svelte while in there.

findings.title: Back-arrow targets contradict the tab IA: Documents and More eject to the trips list, siblings return to trip home, Today's back crosses the mode boundary
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/documents/+page.svelte:65 and more/+page.svelte (backHref="/trips"); members/+page.svelte:96 and phases/+page.svelte:42 (backHref=trip home); goals, lists, expenses, budget +page.svelte (backHref="/trips"); today/+page.svelte:39-45 (backHref="/trips/{slug}" — planning Overview); nav-tabs.ts:22-38 (the five tabs are BottomNav peers)
findings.what: Pages that are sibling tabs in the BottomNav disagree about what 'back' means. Itinerary home, Money, Docs, More, Goals and Lists back out to /trips (the cross-trip list); Members and Phases back to trip home. Within one SubTabs row (Overview/Phases/Lists/Goals) the targets differ: Overview/Goals/Lists exit the trip, Phases returns home. Worst two: (a) Documents — a top-level tab in BOTH modes and the offline boarding-pass surface — has a back arrow that exits the trip entirely; one reflexive tap mid-trip while looking for a ticket dumps you on the trips list. (b) Today (Trip Mode) backs to the planning Overview, silently crossing the D3 mode boundary without the pill, landing planning content inside trip chrome.
findings.why: M4 — the expected link (consistent parent) is missing; the same gesture teleports to three different altitudes. Designer lens: the IA can't be learned because the hierarchy is encoded inconsistently — tabs are simultaneously siblings (BottomNav) and children of different parents (back arrows). For the novice lens the back arrow is the most-trusted control on the screen; mid-trip misfires on Documents are scenario-19 friction at the worst moment.
findings.fix: One rule: top-level tab pages show no back arrow (they're peers — the BottomNav/SideRail is the navigation); only true sub-pages (item, day, goal detail, settings, inbox) show back, always to their in-trip parent. Trip-mode pages never back into planning surfaces — the mode pill is the only mode door (D3).

findings.title: Notification bell appears on 6 pages and vanishes on the rest — including Trip Mode's home and every page a notification links to
findings.where: Bell present: trips/[slug]/+page.svelte:62, today/+page.svelte:47, today/upcoming/+page.svelte:37, expenses/+page.svelte:88, budget/+page.svelte:91, more/+page.svelte:31. Absent: now/+page.svelte:21 (Trip Mode home), members/+page.svelte:96, documents/+page.svelte:65-71 ('On trip' pill instead), inbox/+page.svelte:41, items/[itemId]/+page.svelte:54. Link targets: backend/pb_hooks/notifications.pb.js:56 (/inbox), :116 (/items/{id}), :169 (/members). Data is already loaded trip-wide at trips/[slug]/+layout.server.ts:41-48
findings.what: The bell is hand-mounted per page rather than living in shared chrome, so it exists on six pages and is missing from the rest. Notably absent on: Now — the default view of an active trip, so the mid-trip awareness surface is gone exactly when the group is most active; Members and Docs — two of the five top-level tabs; and all three pages notifications link TO (/inbox, /items/[id], /members), so following one notification hides the rest of the queue. The layout already loads notifications+unreadCount for every trip page — the data rides along unused. (SPEC_BACKLOG.md:83-84 defers realtime badge updates and dedup; placement is not deferred anywhere.)
findings.why: V2 / scenario 42 ('catch up on what changed since I last looked'): for non-owners the bell is the only changes surface (Inbox is owner-gated suggestions), and it's invisible from Trip Mode's home and from half the planning surfaces — shipped but buried (P2 definition). Designer lens: persistent chrome that appears and disappears per tab breaks the contract that the header is stable; users can't form a habit around an intermittent affordance.
findings.fix: Move the bell into shared chrome — render it from AppShell/NavBar via the already-loaded layout data (right-slot default) so every in-trip page gets it, including Now and notification landing pages. Per-page right-snippets (e.g. Documents' 'On trip' pill) compose next to it.

findings.title: Chrome doesn't know where you are: false 'Now'/'Itinerary' highlights on non-tab pages, planning content inside trip chrome on post-join landing, stale view-transition route set
findings.where: src/lib/shell/nav-tabs.ts:48 (trip-mode getActiveTab falls back to 'now'), :54 (planning falls back to 'itinerary'); src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:8-14 (bottomNavRoutes omits documents and both trip-mode tabs); join/[token]/+page.server.ts:98 and invite/[code]/+page.server.ts:97 (accept → /trips/{slug}); AppShell.svelte:35-40 (active trip defaults mode='trip')
findings.what: Three drift symptoms of the same problem — nav metadata hardcoded separately from nav-tabs.ts. (1) getActiveTab's fallback claims a tab on pages that have none: during an active trip, opening Members via the member_joined notification (notifications.pb.js:169) or Expenses via the Add sheet shows Trip-Mode tabs with 'Now' lit while you're on neither. (2) Joining an active trip lands on /trips/{slug} — the planning Overview rendered inside Trip-Mode chrome (Now/Today/Add/Docs) with 'Now' highlighted; the new member's very first screen (D2) has nav that contradicts the content and no tab pointing at the page they're on. (3) The view-transition classifier's bottomNavRoutes set predates v4: Docs (a tab in both modes) animates as drill-down, and Now↔Today taps animate as 'peer' instead of 'tab', so the motion grammar (tab=slide, drill=zoom) lies about the same hierarchy the highlights lie about.
findings.why: V3 seam-flavored: the planning→trip-day seam is exactly where chrome and content disagree — a mid-trip joiner's first five minutes happen in a mixed-mode UI. Designer lens: active-tab state and motion are the two systems that teach users the IA; both report false positions. Per-symptom impact is small (P3) but it's systematic.
findings.fix: Single source of truth: derive both the transition route set and active-tab matching from getNavConfig's hrefs instead of parallel hardcoded lists; make getActiveTab return null (no highlight) when nothing matches; land mid-trip joiners on /trips/{slug}/now when the trip is active (one conditional in the two accept actions).

findings.title: No +error.svelte anywhere — every 404/403/500 is a chrome-less dead end
findings.where: src/routes/ (no +error.svelte at any level; no src/error.html); throwers: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+layout.server.ts:12,26; src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/inbox/+page.server.ts:11; items/[itemId]/+page.server.ts:15-19; archive/[token]/+page.server.ts:21-34
findings.what: Zero error boundaries exist in the route tree (verified by find for +error.svelte and absence of src/error.html). Every error() throw — 'Trip not found', 'You are not a member of this trip', 'Item not found', 'Archive not found', plus any uncaught 500 — renders SvelteKit's bare default page: status code, message text, no Waypoint chrome, no link anywhere. The 403 even discloses nothing actionable.
findings.why: M2 one-way street on every error path. Power-user walk: a bookmarked/shared deep link to a trip you were removed from, or a notification link to a since-deleted item (notifications.pb.js:116 links to /trips/{slug}/items/{id} with no tombstone handling), lands on a white page whose only exit is browser back. Mid-trip on a phone PWA (no browser chrome in standalone mode) this is a literal dead end.
findings.fix: Add a root src/routes/+error.svelte with app styling and a 'Back to your trips' link (mirror the pattern invite/[code]/+page.svelte:36-48 already uses for its not_found state); optionally a (app)/trips/[slug]/+error.svelte that keeps the AppShell. Add src/error.html for pre-render failures.

findings.title: Deep links are destroyed at the login wall — no return-URL preservation
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts:5-7 (redirect(303, '/login') with no ?next param); src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.server.ts:46 (verifyOTP hard-redirects to /claim); claim/+page.server.ts:22,38 (falls through to /trips)
findings.what: Any authenticated route opened while logged out redirects to /login discarding the original URL. After OTP verification the user is force-routed /login → /claim → /trips. A link to /trips/paris-2026/items/abc123 (the exact artifact-link Waypoint is supposed to own) ends at the trips list with the destination gone.
findings.why: M4 expected-but-missing link mechanics, failing V1 for the power persona: the only way to reach the shared item after authing is to go back to the group text and tap the link a second time — the old stack is literally the recovery path. New device / expired session makes this routine, not edge-case.
findings.fix: (app)/+layout.server.ts: redirect(303, `/login?next=${encodeURIComponent(url.pathname + url.search)}`); login verifyOTP redirects to a same-origin-validated next (falling back to /claim). /claim should pass next through its no-claims redirect.

findings.title: /account is unreachable from inside a trip — the only inbound link is the trips-list header
findings.where: Inbound: src/routes/(app)/trips/+page.svelte:37 (sole /account link app-wide, verified by grep). Absent from: src/lib/shell/components/SideRail.svelte (no account/profile entry), nav-tabs.ts:18-41 (no tab), trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte (no card). Back link hardcoded: src/routes/(app)/account/+page.svelte:56 (backHref="/trips")
findings.what: Profile (name/avatar, shipped per #104/PRD #59) is linked from exactly one place: the /trips list header. Inside a trip — where members live — neither the More page, the desktop SideRail, nor any nav surface offers it. The natural guesses (More tab; rail footer) are wrong-guess points. /account's back button always dumps you at /trips even if you came from mid-trip.
findings.why: M4 expected-but-missing link; charter scenario 41 names exactly this ('/account reachability from where you'd expect'). Power walk from /trips/x/today: ModePill→Edit plan→More→(nothing)→back out to /trips→avatar icon→/account→back→re-enter trip→re-navigate = ~7 taps with 1-2 wrong guesses, against an expected 2.
findings.fix: Add a 'Profile' card to trips/[slug]/more/+page.svelte (ungated section) and an avatar/account entry at the SideRail bottom; make account's NavBar back use history (or accept a ?from= param) instead of hardcoded /trips.

findings.title: Notification bell renders on only 6 of ~20 trip routes — including missing from Trip-Mode home (Now)
findings.where: Data loaded for every trip page: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/+layout.server.ts:41-48. Rendered only in: trips/[slug]/+page.svelte:62, today/+page.svelte:47, today/upcoming, expenses/+page.svelte:88, budget/+page.svelte:91, more/+page.svelte:31. Absent: now/+page.svelte:21 (NavBar has no right snippet), members, goals, lists, documents, days/[dayId], items/[itemId], phases, parking-lot, settings, inbox, and /trips list
findings.what: Notifications are fetched by the trip layout on every page, but the bell is a per-page NavBar snippet pasted into six pages. It vanishes on the rest — most glaringly on /now, the Trip-Mode home tab (its sibling tab /today has it), and on the members page, the very route member_joined notifications (notifications.pb.js:169) link to: tapping that notification lands you somewhere the bell no longer exists. There is also no all-notifications surface anywhere; the dropdown is the only reader. (Badge-not-live is documented-deferred, SPEC_BACKLOG.md:84 — not flagged.)
findings.why: M3 shipped-but-partially-unreachable: the data ships on every route, the UI on six. Fails V2-adjacent scenario 42 ('catch up on what changed') for a power user, who must navigate to a bell-bearing page before they can even see the unread badge; the inconsistency reads as state loss.
findings.fix: Move NotificationBell into shared chrome — AppShell (src/lib/shell/components/AppShell.svelte) or NavBar default — fed from the already-loaded layout data, so every trip route shows it; keep the per-page right-snippet override for immersive routes.

findings.title: 'Skip for now' on /claim is one-way — pending claims and invites become unreachable until next login
findings.where: Entry points to /claim: only (auth)/login/+page.server.ts:46 and api/dev/login/+server.ts:39 (grep-verified). Skip: claim/+page.server.ts:78-80. Pending-invite check only runs when claims are empty: claim/+page.server.ts:27-38. /trips surfaces nothing: trips/+page.server.ts has zero claim/pending logic (grep-verified)
findings.what: The claim screen (placeholder memberships + pending email invites) appears exactly once per login. 'Skip for now' redirects to /trips, and no route, banner, card, or menu entry links back to /claim; navigating to /claim manually works but nothing tells you it exists. Pending email invites are even narrower — checked only when placeholder claims are empty. A skipper's only in-app recovery is logout→login.
findings.why: M2 one-way street on the membership front door. Power walk: 'I skipped at 7am, now I want to claim my spot' → no path; the realistic recovery is asking the owner to re-send the link via the group text (V1, old stack). Front-door adjacency keeps this at the top of P2.
findings.fix: On /trips load, fetch my-claims/my-pending counts and render a 'You have a pending trip invite' banner linking to /claim; cheap alternative: a 'Pending invites' row on /account or the trips-list header.

findings.title: Trip Settings shows members the full owner console — rejection arrives only after submit
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/trips/[slug]/settings/+page.server.ts:38-39,67-68,97-98,133-134 (role checked only inside actions, all returning fail(403)); +page.svelte has no role gating (grep for role/isOwner matches only role="alert"); reached ungated via more/+page.svelte:72 card visible to every member ('Trip details, slug, auto-approve, danger zone')
findings.what: A plain member opens Settings from More and sees fully enabled forms: trip title/dates, slug, auto-approve, archive controls, and the delete-trip danger zone. Every input accepts edits; only on POST does 'Only trip owners can change settings.' appear. Contrast: the More page itself hides Inbox/Closeout/Clone cards by role (more/+page.svelte:36,54,88), so the app's own role-gating convention is editable-equals-allowed.
findings.why: Vision/role-gating UX failure (V2 flavor: invite a contribution, then slam the door); inconsistent with the established hide-by-role pattern one screen earlier. Power user wastes a filled form; a member can walk the delete-trip confirmation before being refused.
findings.fix: Pass membership.role into settings page data and render read-only values plus an 'Owners manage settings' note for non-owners (or gate the load like inbox/+page.server.ts:11 and hide the More card to match the other owner cards).

findings.title: View-transition route classifier is stale — Docs/Now/Today tab switches animate as drills, not tabs
findings.where: src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:8-14 (bottomNavRoutes set: [slug], expenses, budget, members, more) vs src/lib/shell/nav-tabs.ts:22-39 (actual tabs: planning = itinerary/expenses/members/documents/more; trip = now/today/documents)
findings.what: The transition classifier's hardcoded tab set predates the Documents tab (#71) and Trip Mode tabs. Itinerary→Docs (adjacent bottom-nav taps) classifies as drill-down; Now↔Today classifies as peer; budget — no longer a tab — still gets tab treatment from expenses. The 'tab' animation fires only between the four legacy planning tabs.
findings.why: M4-style drift between two sources of truth for the same chrome; designer-axis inconsistency the power user feels as the app 'zooming into' a sibling tab. Will silently re-break on every future tab change.
findings.fix: Derive the tab set from getNavConfig() route ids (single source of truth in nav-tabs.ts) instead of a hand-maintained Set in (app)/+layout.svelte; include trip-mode tabs.

findings.title: /login has no 'Resend code' — the invite and join OTP forms both have one
findings.where: src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.svelte:110-118 (only escape is 'Use a different email', which resets to step 1); contrast join/[token]/+page.svelte:222 and invite/[code]/+page.svelte:239 ('Resend code' buttons)
findings.what: If the OTP email is slow or lost, the main login's only affordance abandons the flow back to the email field for a full re-entry; the same inline OTP pattern on /invite and /join ships a one-tap Resend. Three implementations of the identical flow, the most-used one is the worst.
findings.why: Friction/inconsistency on the most-trafficked auth surface; power user pays 3 taps + retype for what is 1 tap on the front-door pages. (No SPEC_BACKLOG/PRD deferral found for login resend.)
findings.fix: Port the Resend button from join/[token]/+page.svelte:222 into login's verify step — re-posts ?/requestOTP with the retained email and swaps otpId.
